task_completion.visual_evidence: A work item of type Epic with the title 'Mobile App Experience' is visible in the Azure DevOps interface.

task_completion.description: Features 'User Authentication' and 'Product Catalog Browsing' are created
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two work items of type Feature with the titles 'User Authentication' and 'Product Catalog Browsing' are visible.

task_completion.description: Features are parented to the Epic
task_completion.visual_evidence: A hierarchical view (e.g., Backlogs tree view) shows the two Features nested under the 'Mobile App Experience' Epic, or the Features' parent link explicitly points to the Epic.

task_completion.description: Four specific User Stories are created
task_completion.visual_evidence: Work items of type User Story are visible with titles containing 'log in with my email', 'reset my password', 'browse products by category', and 'search products by keyword'.

task_completion.description: User Stories are correctly parented to their respective Features
task_completion.visual_evidence: A hierarchical view shows the 'log in' and 'reset password' stories nested under the 'User Authentication' Feature, and the 'browse' and 'search' stories nested under the 'Product Catalog Browsing' Feature.

task_completion.description: Story points and priorities are correctly assigned to the items
task_completion.visual_evidence: The backlog view or individual item views show Story Points (5, 3, 8, 5) and Priorities (1, 2, 1, 2) matching the task description for the respective User Stories and Features.

integrity.description: The agent used the Azure DevOps web interface to create the work items
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Azure DevOps web interface (boards, backlogs, or work item forms) rather than terminal commands or script outputs.

integrity.description: The work items are created as distinct entities in the system
integrity.visual_evidence: The items appear as separate rows in a backlog or query, not just text typed into a single description, wiki page, or document.

integrity.description: The hierarchy is established using the application's built-in parent-child relationship features
integrity.visual_evidence: The hierarchy is shown via the system's native tree view expand/collapse icons or the 'Links' tab, not just manually indented text.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
Project: TailwindTraders.
Epic: 'Mobile App Experience' (Priority: 1).
Feature 1: 'User Authentication' (Priority: 1, Parent: Mobile App Experience).
- Story 1: '...log in with my email...' (Points: 5, Priority: 1, Parent: User Authentication).
- Story 2: '...reset my password...' (Points: 3, Priority: 2, Parent: User Authentication).
Feature 2: 'Product Catalog Browsing' (Priority: 2, Parent: Mobile App Experience).
- Story 3: '...browse products by category...' (Points: 8, Priority: 1, Parent: Product Catalog Browsing).
- Story 4: '...search products by keyword...' (Points: 5, Priority: 2, Parent: Product Catalog Browsing).
